title: elymus lachnophyllus
intro: Elymus lachnophyllus, also known as Wild Rye or Prickly Wheat Grass, is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of China and parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Elymus lachnophyllus include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and boosting the immune system. Elymus lachnophyllus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal use.
